project_name: C. elegans bacteria metabolite neural analysis
current_phase: The filtered 202604 review has completed model-diagnosis interpretation through shared-structure localization. Current conclusion: QC20 log2 Euclidean is the best accepted broad fixed baseline, within-date RSA is stronger on average, cross-date is mixed rather than uniformly bad, base odors argue against a dominant global date artifact, and reliable-pair/localization results support partial date-sensitive chemical-neural overlap. The supervised chemical subspace and metabolite subset search is now archived as a negative route: selected models collapse to 1-2 chemicals, final all-sample RSA is only moderate, and validation-scope complexity makes the line hard to interpret. Preserve its outputs for audit, but do not continue extending this route. The taxonomy class stability figure pass has now been run with fixed-class permutation, reselection stability, RDM comparison, a class-to-class chemical RDM RSA matrix, and a summary scorecard as main display layers; full-search permutation is retained as diagnostic-only search-correction output. The RDM comparison should show full-stimulus raw-value `magma` heatmaps for neural, full chemical, and top-class chemical RDMs without pairwise-rank panels, and should also be mirrored into date_controlled_rsa_review as a companion foundation figure. Current outputs are exploratory figures, not paper-ready results.
active_tasks: Decide whether non-ASE L/R merge plus trial-median neural correlation should replace the older trial-mean neural reference for filtered-batch exploratory reporting
Compare QC-filtered log2 Euclidean, direct correlation, and the current control on the filtered 202604 batch across per-date, pooled all-pairs, and within-date-only pooled RSA summaries
Review generated taxonomy class stability figures and decide which classes to keep as stable exploratory candidates
Decide whether the filtered 202604_without_20260331 batch should replace the original 202604 batch for downstream reporting and geometry
Confirm the raw-to-matrix preprocessing pipeline with the collaborator before changing the default metabolite distance interpretation beyond exploratory review

blockers: The raw-to-matrix preprocessing pipeline is still not confirmed by the collaborator, which weakens the theoretical case for CLR/Aitchison on matrix.xlsx
Neither the original 202604 batch nor the filtered 202604_without_20260331 comparison batch has a matching geometry rerun yet for pooled Stage 2-style diagnostics
The filtered rerun without 20260331 improves pooled reliability and RSA, but its LODO metric is unavailable, so date-level generalization cannot be reported from that comparison run
Supervised neural-aligned modeling is not yet implemented; training-set predicted RDMs would be leakage, so main evidence must come from held-out date and held-out-stimulus predictions
The earlier taxonomy shortlist was selected under an older mean-based neural reference, so those subspace rankings cannot be mixed directly with current trial-median figures
The 202603 date panels remain disjoint, so within-date geometry stability is overlap-limited and pooled prototype interpretations remain descriptive only
notes: Use docs/stage1-reliability-explained.md and docs/stage2-geometry-exploration-summary.md as the human-readable explainers. Versioned data layout archives the prior batch under data/202601 and results/202601. The original 202604 run uses data/202604/202604_data.parquet, with preprocess outputs at data/202604/202604_preprocess, reliability outputs at results/202604/reliability, and direct RSA outputs at results/202604/rsa. A comparison rerun that excludes the problematic 20260331 date is also preserved at data/202604/202604_data_without_20260331.parquet, with preprocess outputs at data/202604/202604_preprocess_without_20260331 and analysis outputs under results/202604_without_20260331. The full 202604 batch contains 1397 trials, 64 stimuli, 23 individuals, and 5 dates; the filtered rerun contains 1081 trials, 52 stimuli, 19 individuals, and 4 dates. Removing 20260331 improves pooled response_window reliability and direct RSA, but removes usable LODO support. A 2026-04-19 raw-vs-matrix recheck found that matrix.xlsx is better interpreted as a feature-centered ratio-like signal than as raw abundance, and that the raw workbook QCRSD column uses fractional thresholds where 0.2 and 0.3 mean 20% and 30% RSD. The earlier pooled review package under results/202604_without_20260331/rsa_distance_method_comparison still shows Aitchison above the current production correlation contract, but a later temporary non-Aitchison recheck found that pooled log2(matrix)+Euclidean with QCRSD<=0.2 is stronger than the current control in both response_window and full_trajectory, while QC alone leaves the current control essentially unchanged and per-date means remain mixed. Current low pooled RSA should be interpreted as partial, local, date-sensitive chemical-neural overlap rather than complete global RDM alignment. The taxonomy class stability review under results/202604_without_20260331/taxonomy_class_stability_review is exploratory and currently points to Purine nucleosides as the strongest and most stable class candidate. Main figures should use fixed-class permutation, reselection stability, the RDM comparison, and the summary scorecard; the RDM comparison should be a three-panel raw-value `magma` heatmap showing all 52 stimuli for neural response-window RDM, full QC20 chemical RDM with 267 retained metabolites, and the top-class chemical RDM, without within-RDM scaling or pairwise-rank/scatter subplots, and should also be mirrored into date_controlled_rsa_review/figures as `neural_chemical_rdm_foundation__response_window_full_vs_top_class_rdms.png`; the full-search permutation histogram should remain diagnostic-only because its q99 line is slightly above the observed best RSA despite the empirical search-corrected p=0.012994. Weighted fusion scope matters: best_weighted_fusion_fixed_weights is 0.251296 for response-window all-pairs and 0.346179 for response-window within-date, while full-trajectory is 0.286738 all-pairs and 0.395254 within-date; remembered ~0.37 values refer to date-controlled within/stratified-within style summaries, not response-window all-pairs. Reliability now also writes one per-date focus-view same-vs-different figure per available date under results/<run>/reliability/figures/same_vs_different_by_date__*.png and pooled/per-date per-stimulus same-vs-different figures under results/<run>/reliability/figures/per_stimulus_same_vs_different__*.png to make weak dates and weak stimuli easier to spot. Geometry has not been rerun for either 202604 variant yet, so pooled Stage 2-style diagnostics still point to the earlier 202603 geometry package. The filtered-batch model-diagnosis package now also writes anchor_localization, anchor_localization_summary, and anchor_triplet_summary tables plus anchor_rank_alignment, anchor_neighborhood_overlap, localization_hotspots figures, and a Shared Structure section in run_summary.md for the three target models. The neutral analysis contract is merged to master, so public APIs and default output roots use reliability/geometry/rsa naming rather than stage labels.

current_neural_reference_review.reference: non-ASE L/R merge + trial median + correlation distance

distance_method_review.review_reference: older pooled chemistry-method scan with the default mean-based neural reference

highlighted_triple_union.combo: Class::Indoles and derivatives + SubClass::Pyridinecarboxylic acids and derivatives + SuperClass::Organic oxygen compounds

best_trial_median_triple.combo: Class::Indoles and derivatives + SuperClass::Organic oxygen compounds + Class::Purine nucleosides

filtered_without_20260331.status_note: The filtered comparison rerun without 20260331 improves pooled reliability and RSA versus the full 202604 batch. The current strongest non-mined exploratory pairing is non-ASE L/R-merged trial-median neural correlation against QCRSD<=0.2 log2 Euclidean chemistry, with the broad global benchmark at 0.195004 / 0.222131 versus 0.133997 / 0.164824 for the current chemical control under the same neural reference. Earlier taxonomy shortlist results are now known to be neural-reference-dependent: the old highlighted triple union drops under the current median reference and is no longer near-optimal. LODO is unavailable and geometry has not been rerun.

current_outputs.status_note: The full 202604 outputs remain intact, and a filtered comparison rerun without 20260331 is now recorded alongside them. Reliability includes per-date same-vs-different figures plus pooled/per-date per-stimulus same-vs-different figures; geometry has not been rerun for either variant yet.

stage_handoff.stage1_interpretation: Stimulus identity structure is real and reproducible, but date-level generalization is weaker than individual-level generalization.
stage_handoff.stage2_interpretation: Pooled neural geometry is real and highly consistent across response_window and full_trajectory, but group-level stability remains overlap-limited and should be treated as supplementary rather than primary evidence.

stage3_default_inputs.metabolite_distance_status: under_review_after_distance-method comparison; current log1p-plus-correlation remains the tentative default

stage3_focus: Define the Stage 3 model space from data/matrix.xlsx or a curated feature table
Run pooled RSA first rather than starting from group-level RSA
Use response_window as the formal neural RDM and full_trajectory as sensitivity analysis

real_data_dry_run.default_runtime_note: The 202603 Stage 3 pipeline now runs successfully with data/model_space_202603 and 1000 permutations; prototype supplementary outputs were validated before the feature worktree was cleaned.
real_data_dry_run.interpretation_note: For the 202603 batch, global_profile remains the top primary model in both response_window and full_trajectory. Interpretation should still note that only global_profile and bile_acid have populated primary-model inputs and that the two dates use disjoint stimulus panels, which limits pooled prototype figures to descriptive use.

prototype_supplement_branch.next_action: No worktree action remains; continue follow-up work from master

stage3_risks: A strong pooled neural-model match could still overstate biological stability if group-level overlap limitations are ignored
Model RDM conclusions will only be as good as the stimulus-to-metabolite mapping quality
Stage 3 should avoid treating grouped RSA as primary evidence unless overlap support is materially improved
Pooled prototype paired RDM figures for 202603 must remain clearly marked as descriptive because the two dates share no stimuli
matrix.xlsx is not yet confirmed to be compositional relative abundance, so CLR/Aitchison-based interpretation should remain cautious until the collaborator clarifies the upstream preprocessing
